requiredRows.requiredObservation: Parent and child devices show the same trusted managed link, source key id, target child profile, allowed scopes, and locked trusted-update mode.
Parent sends a signed keyword policy and the child applies the newer revision through managed validation, with blocked keyword behavior visible after refresh.
Parent sends a signed channel policy and the child applies it through the same managed channel blocking path as local controls.
Parent sends a signed video policy and the child applies it through the managed video blocking path.
Parent changes Main/Kids viewing-space policy and the child route gate enforces the new policy before managed YouTube content remains usable.
Parent sends a signed time-limit policy and the child compiles/enforces it, including reduced-budget or zero-budget timeout behavior.
When transport becomes unavailable, the child keeps the last accepted parent policy active and records a safe unavailable state.
A stale, replayed, revoked-link, wrong-key, or wrong-target policy is rejected and does not weaken the last accepted policy.
Parent-visible action history records accepted and rejected outcomes without plaintext rules, PINs, private keys, mailbox ciphertext, or decrypted payloads.

The parent command center shows sync readiness and remote conflict state as compact status chips, visually marks conflict rows, and Review Conflict opens protected history only after parent/account authority.

Parent rotates the managed source signing key, old child-device links are marked key-revoked, the command center shows affected profiles as needing re-pairing, and protected history records trust_link.key_revoke without plaintext rules or private key material.
With no eligible provider or no pending policy, YouTube pages remain in no-work state: no added observers, timers, DOM scans, JSON work, or LAN fetch loops.

Managed action history may preserve only sanitizer-accepted encrypted summary metadata and privacy markers; the artifact must not record plaintext rules, raw policy JSON, decrypted payloads, raw ciphertext, private keys, PINs, or passwords.

The parent command center Delivery detail distinguishes Send Update, Same-Home Pickup, Later Pickup, paired-but-pickup-pending, revoked, stale, and no-device states without exposing plaintext rules or private key material.

Parent imports, checks, or refreshes a managed channel list, applies only parent-approved list-derived channel rules to the protected profile, and sends the resulting signed channel policy through the same verified-device delivery path as manual channel edits.
